pages.title: Home
pages.content: Realization often comes in the most unexpected ways. We are an independent design studio focused on branding, visual identity, creative direction, print, title sequences, animation, interactive, etc. We have developed a network of creatives that span a variety of disciplines in which we partner to meet the specific needs of any project. Intentionally small, truly global with an intent to create the highest level of excellence in our work.

2024 Reel

An overview of multidiciplinary work created that covers brand identity, brand strategy, creative direction, interactive design, and overarching brand narrative. All pulled through different lenses.

pages.title: Billion Dollar Code
pages.content:
Billion Dollar Code

A Netflix original series. The plot follows two interconnected timelines. In Berlin of the 1990s a group of young hackers and art students had developed a planet browser in 1991 which was used by Joachim Sauter to get funding from Deutsche Telekom in 1993 to start the successful development of Terravision.

In 2014 a patent infringement dispute against Google, alleging that TerraVision was used to develop Google Earth. The series dramatized the real lawsuit which lasted until 2017.

Studio role _ Identity development. Main and Teaser Keyart. Motion Studies. Guidelin

pages.title: Squarespace Rebrand
pages.content:
Squarespace Rebrand

A yearlong project in partnership with DIA. We spent the first 3 months in a deep dive of the brand, understanding what makes the company tick. Who and what is Squaresapce and how could we redevelop the brand in a way that allowed it to stay relevant for years to come. We developed a robust system that was fueled by Kinetic Typography. This was central and foundational to the work developed. We looked at an audio Mnemonic, a jingle as something memoriable. Surely, as a digital ( screen-first ) company...motion should be top of mind.

Studio Role _ Identity Development. Motion Studies.
pages.excerpt:
Squarespace Rebrand


A yearlong project in partnership with DIA. We spent the first 3 months in a deep dive of the brand, understanding what makes the...

pages.title: Crunchyroll Rebrand
pages.content:
Crunchyroll Rebrand

A year in the making. We were brought into help rebrand Cruncyroll with a specific challenge. Future proof the brand so that it’s relevant for the next 10 years. A daunting task that uncovered some new approaches to how we look at brand development. We discovered that glyphs could provide authenticity of Anime culture for a fanbaase exceptionally picky.

We realized that we had developed a trojan horse of sorts for the brand. That by adopting a broad language of ever developing Glyphs ( which Japanese designers can continue to add to ) the brand will continue to grow and develop.

Studio Role _ Identity Development. Motion Studies.

Crunchyroll
 Rebrand

pages.title: How to Sell Drugs Online (Fast)
pages.content:
How to Sell Drugs Online (Fast)

How to Sell Drugs Online (Fast) is set in fictional Rinseln, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany, and told from the perspective of high-school outcast and nerd Moritz Zimmermann. The series follows Moritz and his friend Lenny Sander attempting to rekindle Moritz's love for ex-girlfriend Lisa Novak, through selling ecstasy online.

Starting off as a small time business, it quickly spirals out of control and Moritz and Lenny learn to deal with the consequences of large scale drug trafficking.

The series is inspired by a true story that took place in Leipzig in 2015. Maximilian Schmidt, the inspiration for the television series, was sentenced to seven years in prison.[5]

Studio Role _ Identity Development. Main and Teaser Keyart. Series Announcement.

As the thrillist appropriately says “You'll never look at kittens the same way again after watching this freaky limited series.“

pages.title: Finestkind
pages.content:
Finestkind

Working closely with Paramount Studios we conceived and developed the Identity and keyart for the 2024 film release Finestkind. Written and directed by Brian Heldeland and starring Ben Foster, Toby Wallace, Jenna Ortegg, and Tommy Lee Jones.

The storyline finds two estranged brothers who hatch a deal with a Boston crime syndicate, which spells danger for the brothers and their father as well as a mysterious young woman.
Studio Role _ Identity Development, Main Key Art, Character Key Art

pages.title: BONN
pages.content:
Beasts of No Nation

A Criterion Collection Release. Beasts of No Nation is a 2015 American war drama film written, co-produced, shot, and directed by Cary Joji Fukunaga.

It follows a young boy who becomes a child soldier as his country experiences a horrific civil war. Shot in Ghana and starring Idris Elba, Abraham Attah, Ama K. Abebrese, Grace Nortey, David Dontoh, and Opeyemi Fagbohungbe, the film is based on the 2005 novel of the same name by Uzodinma Iweala, the book itself being named after a Fela Kuti album.

Studio Role _ Cover Art. Identity Development.

pages.title: Biohackers
pages.content:
Biohackers

Studio RoleBiohackers is a German series created by Christian Ditter. It tells a highly-gripping story about friendship, love and revenge at a German University that is overshadowed by a revolutionary biohacking-technology and its ethical implications.

WTF is Biohacking? It’s a very broad concept. It ranges from people that drink bulletproof coffee or people that have chips implanted in their body, to people that use at-home DNA analysis kits. Biohackers often associate themselves with biopunk, transhumanism or techno-progressivism. Some biohackers are mostly focused on improving their performance, whether it be physically or at work.

Studio Role _Identity Developement. Naming. Main and Teaser Keyart.

pages.title: Queen's Gambit
pages.content:
The Queen’s Gambit

The Queen's Gambit follows the life of an orphan chess prodigy, Elizabeth Harmon, during her quest to become an elite chess player while struggling with emotional problems, drugs and alcohol dependency. The title of the series refers to a chess opening of the same name. The story is set in the mid-1950s and 1960s.

Studio Role _ Identity Development. Motion Studies. Guidelines.

Griselda

Slated to be one of the larger releases for Netflix in 2024, we partnered up to help develop and roll out a full campaign aimed at teasing the provocative subject matter at the heart of Griselda.

For those who don't know, Griselda Blanco was a devoted mother who created one of the most profitable cartels in history.

pages.title: True Story
pages.content:
True Story

A Netflix original series. One of Kevin Hart’s first foray’s into entertainment. True Story is based on the true story of Hart who is named “kid” in the Netflix series. It’s a limited series that focuses on whether Kid can successfully get away with murder, all while sustaining his comedy and acting career, and throwing everyone, even the people closest to him, off the scent. Along the way, there are twists and turns, including one involving Carlton himself.

Studio Role _ Identity Development. Motion Studies.

pages.content:
The Last of Us

A series that captivated our imagination, we set to use this subject manner to explore image generation and more specifically, how we could push typography in new ways. There is an enormous amount of cynicism about what can be done through image generation, we feel pretty positive about it.

pages.title: Vikings Valhalla
pages.content:
Vikings Valhalla

A Netflix original series. In this sequel to "Vikings," a hundred years have passed and a new generation of legendary heroes arises to forge its own destiny.

A challenging assignment as we needed to keep the brand IP intact to ensure we did not alienate the large fanbase of Vikings. Through an exhaustive exploration we were able to interweave a variety of elements to make for an intelligent reapproach.

Studio Role _ Identity Development.

pages.title: The Trial
pages.content:
The Trial

We were honored to have been asked to do the cover for the Kafka / Welles “The Trial” - a visual masterpiece. Almost every frame of the film is a frameable piece of art.

Orson Welles played deftly with perspective, angles, and light throughout the film. As Roger Ebert accurately wrote, the film is masterful for its “disorienting camera angles and unconventional use of focus.” We wanted to create an image that captured the puzzling world that the character Josef K had been imprisoned within. We also wanted to create something that could be viewed from a variety of different angles.

Studio Role _ Identity Development, Key Art, Packaging.

The Trial



We were honored to have been asked to do the cover for the Kafka / Welles “The Trial” - a visual masterpiece. Almost every frame of the...

pages.title: This is not a burial
The Eddy 2
KATLA
pages.content:
KATLA

A Netflix original series. The series focuses on the eruption of the Katla volcano and the handful of people that remain in the nearby town of Vík.

An ash-caked woman who apparently disappeared twenty years earlier appears in the village, looking as she did back then. More ash-caked people who died in the past arrive in Vík, creating a paradox for those who knew them and reviving legends of "changelings".

The series focues on a cast of characters that are forced to cope and deal with events from the past.

Studio Role: Identity Development. Simplified Guidelines.

pages.title: MAID
pages.content:
MAID

Maid is a limited series created for Netflix by Molly Smith Metzler. The series is inspired by Stephanie Land's memoir Maid: Hard Work, Low Pay, and a Mother's Will to Survive.

Its story focuses on a young mother who escapes an abusive relationship, subsequently struggling to provide for her daughter by getting a job cleaning houses.

Studio Role _ Identity Development. Simplified Guidelines.

pages.title: Avatar - The Last Airbender
pages.content:
Avatar - The Last Airbender

Avatar: The Last Airbender is set in a world where human civilization consists of four nations, named after the four classic elements, the Water Tribes, the Earth Kingdom, the Fire Nation, and the Air Nomads. In each nation, certain people, known as "benders" (waterbenders, earthbenders, firebenders, and airbenders), have the ability to telekinetically manipulate and control the element corresponding to their nation, using gestures based on Chinese martial arts. The "Avatar" is the only individual with the ability to bend all four elements.

Studio Role _ Identity Development, Look and Feel

Studio Role: Strategic research and analysis. Naming. Identity Development

pages.title: Unorthodox
Daybreak
pages.content:
Daybreak

The series follows the story of 17-year-old Canadian high school outcast Josh Wheeler, who is searching for his missing British girlfriend Sam Dean in post-apocalyptic Glendale, California. He is joined by a ragtag group of misfits, including 10-year-old pyromaniac Angelica and Josh's former high school bully Wesley, who is now a pacifist would-be samurai. Josh tries to survive among the hordes of Mad Max-style gangs (evil jocks, cheerleaders turned Amazonian warriors, etc), adults who have turned into zombie-like creatures called Ghoulies, and the mysterious Baron Triumph.

Studio Role: Identity Development. Naming. Character Key Art. Look and feel leading into Global Guidelines.
